title: Difference between Arousal & Desire
caption: Do you know the difference?
layout: Two-column comparison. LEFT (heart icon, burgundy header 'AROUSAL', italic subtitle 'a physical response'): bullet list — Physiological response (like increased heart rate); Genital and body focus; Felt in the moment; Often involuntary and reactive. RIGHT (shield icon, teal header 'DESIRE', italic subtitle 'a mental and emotional yearning'): bullets — Subjective and emotional; Cognitive and deep-seated; Felt over time and in anticipation; Often proactive and intentional. Bottom band 'LOVEMAKING' in burgundy: Arousal can spark Desire / Desire can shape Arousal / Understanding both is key to intimacy.

title: Spontaneous vs Responsive desire
caption: Is your desire responsive or spontaneous? Both are valid and healthy.
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'SPONTANEOUS') subtitle 'wants sex out of nowhere': Triggered by thought or fantasy; Common in early relationships; 'I want it before anything happens'; More common pattern for him. RIGHT (shield, teal 'RESPONSIVE') subtitle 'wants sex once warmed up': Triggered by safety, touch, presence; Common in long-term love and after 40; 'I didn't want it until I started'; More common pattern for her. Bottom band: 'BOTH ARE NORMAL. Most women over 40 are responsive. What are you responding to?'

title: The 4 phases of diminishing desire after 40
caption: Is your desire disappearing? It's common, but not a rule.
layout: Vertical 4-step timeline with hearts as numbered bullets. 1. The slow goodbye ('I don't want it the way I used to'). 2. The quiet shutdown ('I feel nothing when he reaches'). 3. The shame loop ('something must be wrong with me'). 4. The remembering ('she's still in there'). Bottom band: 'This isn't an ending. It's a doorway.'

title: Performance sex vs Connected sex
caption: Which one are you having?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'PERFORMANCE'): Faking enjoyment; Watching yourself from outside; Goal: orgasm; Leaves you depleted. RIGHT (shield, teal 'CONNECTED'): Feeling what's actually here; Inside your own body; Goal: connection and pleasure; Leaves you both feeling loved. Bottom: 'Most women I work with don't have low desire. They have low capacity to perform.'

title: What real foreplay is
caption: Did you know this?
layout: 2x2 quadrant. TOP-LEFT (mental, heart): 'Conversations during the day. Texts. Being thought of.' TOP-RIGHT (emotional, heart): 'Feeling safe. Feeling seen. Feeling heard.' BOTTOM-LEFT (physical, heart): 'Touch with no agenda. Eye contact. Breath.' BOTTOM-RIGHT (energetic, heart): 'Slowing down. Presence. Holding space.' Bottom band: 'Foreplay starts hours before the bedroom.'

title: Desire drainers vs desire builders
caption: What's draining yours?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'DRAINERS'): Mental load; Resentment; Criticism; Pressure; Loneliness; Sex as a chore. RIGHT (shield, teal 'BUILDERS'): Being seen; Slow time; Safety; Curiosity; Play; Receiving without earning it. Bottom: 'Desire needs presence, connection, love.'

title: 5 love languages of midlife intimacy
caption: Which speaks to you?
layout: 5 numbered rows, each with a heart bullet. 1. Words that make me feel chosen. 2. Touch with no agenda. 3. Time without distraction. 4. Acts that say 'I thought of you.' 5. Receiving, her allowing him to give. Bottom: 'After 40 the language often changes. Are you still speaking the old one?'

title: Why we keep having the same argument
caption: Are you stuck in this cycle?
layout: Cycle infographic. Top: 'It starts small... a comment, a tone. He pulls away. She feels unseen.' Then two coloured circles. LEFT (burgundy, heart) 'Inside her': Hurt. Loneliness. 'I don't matter.' Reaches, questions, criticises. A protest for connection. RIGHT (teal, shield) 'Inside him': Feels like he's failing. Not good enough. Overwhelmed. Withdraws, shuts down, avoids. Protecting himself. Centre arrow loop label: 'The more she pushes, he withdraws. The more he withdraws, she pushes.' Bottom: 'What actually works: slow it down, share what's underneath, reach instead of react. Underneath the argument is a longing for connection.'

title: The 4 horsemen of relationship death
caption: Are any of these showing up in yours?
layout: 2x2 grid, each panel with a heart icon. 1. Criticism (attacking who they are, not what they did). 2. Contempt (eye-rolls, sarcasm, disgust). 3. Defensiveness (blame-shifting, victim mode). 4. Stonewalling (shutting down, leaving the room). Bottom: 'These predict divorce. The good news, they can all be replaced.'

title: Healthy fight vs unhealthy fight
caption: Which one are you having?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'UNHEALTHY'): About who's right. Past wounds dragged in. 'You always... / You never...' Goal: win. RIGHT (shield, teal 'HEALTHY'): About what's actually hurting. Stays with this moment. 'I feel... when...' Goal: be heard. Bottom: 'It's not about avoiding fights. It's about how you fight.'

title: Repair attempts that work vs the ones that don't
caption: Which do you reach for?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy "DOESN'T LAND"): 'I said sorry, what more do you want?' / 'You're being dramatic' / Buying flowers without acknowledging. RIGHT (shield, teal 'LANDS'): 'I hear you. That hurt.' / Naming what you did. Asking what would help. Slow physical reach. Bottom: "Repair isn't apologising. Repair is meeting them where they are."

title: Connection vs intimacy vs sex
caption: Do you know what you're missing?
layout: 3 stacked or overlapping panels. CONNECTION: feeling each other in everyday life. INTIMACY: being known and wanted. SEX: the body meeting. Bottom: 'You can have one without the others. Most couples have sex without intimacy. That's the loneliness.'

title: The criticism cycle
caption: Have you been here?
layout: Cycle diagram with 5 numbered steps. 1. She says it sharper than she means to. 2. He hears it as 'I'm not enough.' 3. He withdraws. 4. She feels alone again. 5. She speaks even sharper. Centre label: 'It's the cycle, not you.' Bottom: 'You can change the dance and create connection.'

title: What she's saying vs what he's hearing
caption: Sound familiar?
layout: Two columns of dialogue. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'What she said'): 'You never listen.' / 'Why is it always me?' / "I don't feel close to you anymore." RIGHT (shield, teal 'What he heard'): "You're failing me." / "You're not good enough." / "I'm thinking of leaving." Bottom: 'Most arguments are just translation errors between two scared people.'

title: Body shutdown signs vs body opening signs
caption: Are you opening or protecting yourself?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'SHUTDOWN'): Tightness in the chest; Numbness in the pelvis; Held breath; Frozen jaw; "I can't feel anything." RIGHT (shield, teal 'OPENING'): Soft belly; Warmth in the chest; Slow easy breath; Tingling in the hands; 'I feel here.' Bottom: 'Are you opening or closing?'

title: 5 daily body check-ins
caption: Which one would help you most?
layout: 5 numbered rows with heart bullets. 1. Where am I holding tension right now? 2. What's my breath doing? 3. Am I hungry, tired, or feeling something? 4. What does my body want today? 5. What is she trying to tell me? Bottom: 'Two minutes a day rebuilds a relationship with your body so you feel safe and nourished.'

title: Tension vs surrender
caption: Where are you living?
layout: Horizontal spectrum scale with a marker. LEFT end (burgundy heart) TENSION: armoured, performing, controlled, exhausted. RIGHT end (teal shield) SURRENDER: soft, present, receiving, alive. Middle label: 'Most women over 40 are living too far left. Pleasure lives on the right.' Bottom: 'What do you need to shift into surrender?'

title: The numbness map
caption: Where are you on this map?
layout: 4-stage progression with heart bullets. Stage 1: Awake (full sensation). Stage 2: Quiet (less here). Stage 3: Numb (going through motions). Stage 4: Frozen (no signal at all). Bottom: 'You can come back from any stage. Sound, breath, movement help.'

title: Pleasure scale 0 to 10
caption: Where are you today?
layout: Horizontal scale with descriptors at 0, 3, 5, 7, 10. 0 = numb, no signal. 3 = okay, going through it. 5 = warm but distant. 7 = present, enjoying. 10 = fully embodied. Bottom: "Most women I work with haven't been above a 5 in years. That can change in weeks."

title: The 3 phases of midlife awakening
caption: Which phase are you in?
layout: 3-stage flow with hearts. 1. The disturbance: "something's off but I can't name it." 2. The unravelling: "what I built isn't holding me anymore." 3. The reclaiming: "I'm finding her again." Bottom: "This isn't a crisis. You're stepping into your prime."

title: Old self vs becoming self
caption: Who are you becoming?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'OLD SELF'): pleased everyone; gave first; couldn't say no; sex as a chore; small voice. RIGHT (shield, teal 'BECOMING'): pleases herself first; gives from full; clean no, full yes; lives wellness; clear voice. Bottom: "Who is this woman you're becoming?"

title: People-pleaser cycle vs self-trust cycle
caption: Which cycle are you in?
layout: Two cycles side by side. LEFT (heart, burgundy): say yes -> resent it -> collapse -> over-give to fix it -> say yes again. RIGHT (shield, teal): feel -> name it -> choose -> receive -> trust grows. Bottom: "The cycle you're in is the life you're getting."

title: The good girl vs the embodied woman
caption: Which one are you choosing?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'THE GOOD GIRL'): Doesn't ask. Apologises for taking space. Makes herself useful. Earns love. RIGHT (shield, teal 'THE EMBODIED WOMAN'): Asks clearly. Takes the space she needs. Receives. Loves and is loved. Bottom: "You were taught to be the good girl. It's time to claim your womanhood."

title: What you outgrew vs what you're growing into
caption: What are you ready to release?
layout: Two columns. LEFT (heart, burgundy 'OUTGREW'): being everyone's everything; saying yes when you meant no; performing pleasure; "I'm fine." RIGHT (shield, teal 'GROWING INTO'): being your own first priority; clean truth; real pleasure; "here's how I actually am." Bottom: 'What are you choosing?'

title: The 4 betrayals of self
caption: Have you done any of these?
layout: 4 panels with hearts. 1. Saying yes when your body said no. 2. Pretending desire you didn't feel. 3. Becoming small to keep someone comfortable. 4. Calling exhaustion 'commitment.' Bottom: 'The path back, one honest answer at a time.'

title: The 5-minute daily desire practice
caption: Could you start tomorrow?
layout: 5-step vertical flow with heart bullets. 1. Hand on belly, three breaths. 2. Ask: how is my body today? 3. Listen for one honest answer. 4. Touch yourself somewhere kind (not goal-oriented). 5. Say thank you. Bottom: 'Five minutes. Not as a chore. As a coming home.'

title: How to ask for what you want, 4 steps
caption: Could you try this?
layout: 4-step flow with hearts. 1. Notice what you actually want (not what you should want). 2. Name it to yourself first. 3. Say it once, simply. "I'd love it if..." 4. Receive whatever happens without managing his reaction. Bottom: "You're not too much. You're making his life easier."

title: Boundaries map: yes / no / maybe / not yet
caption: Which feels hardest for you?
layout: 2x2 grid. YES (heart): clean, full-bodied. NO (shield): clean, no apology. MAYBE (heart): I don't know yet, give me time. NOT YET (shield): not closed, not open today. Bottom: 'All four are full sentences.'

title: The invisible burdens
caption: How many do you carry?
layout: Numbered list with small hearts beside each. Remembering everyone's appointments. Knowing what's in the fridge. Tracking emotional temperatures. Planning birthdays. Anticipating needs. Carrying the family's mental health. Bottom: "This is the work no one sees. And you've been doing it for years."

title: Your body at 40, 45, 50, 55
caption: Are you using the gifts of menopause?
layout: 4 vertical panels with hearts and short body text. 40: still cycling, energy starts to shift. 45: sleep changes, mood changes, libido might change. 50: cycle ends, identity shakes loose, desire on your own terms. 55: settling into a different woman, if she's been allowed to arrive. Bottom: 'Most exciting time of your life.'
